candidate_summary: Phase-coherence analogy from Jacquard loom punched-card weaving to LLM decoding: per-decoder-position 'card' = sparse mask over vocabulary sub-channels, produced by a small instruction-tokenizer from the prefix; generation requires the card chain to stay phase-locked to the prefix, analogous to mechanical lockstep between card and shuttle in a Jacquard loom.
hit_rule: hit = (kw_count >= 2) OR (cos >= 0.7) OR (judge >= 0.7); kw matches use case-insensitive substring of any content_word in title+snippet

results.title: How Different Tokenization Algorithms Impact LLMs (NDSS BAR 2025)
results.judge_reason: Generic tokenization-impact study; no per-position mask, no instruction-tokenizer phase-locking, no Jacquard analogy.

results.title: How Different Tokenization Algorithms Impact LLMs and Transformer Models for Binary Code
results.judge_reason: UniASM uses instruction-level tokenization for binary code, but no per-position vocabulary-subchannel mask nor phase-coherence requirement; only nominally overlaps on 'instruction tokenizer' phrase.

results.title: Say Anything but This: When Tokenizer Betrays Reasoning in LLMs
results.judge_reason: Discusses tokenizer effects on reasoning via token-ID ambiguity; orthogonal to per-position card-pattern masking with prefix-phase-locking.

results.title: Analysis of Vocabulary and Subword Tokenization (RANLP 2025)
results.judge_reason: Standard subword vocabulary tuning for MT; no instruction-conditioned per-position mask or phase-coherence concept.

results.judge_reason: Overview of BPE/Unigram/WordPiece; no per-position card mask, no instruction tokenizer producing activation masks, no phase coherence.

verdict: Candidate analogy (per-position vocabulary-subchannel mask from instruction-tokenizer phase-locked to prefix) does not appear in retrieved results. The two keyword-triggered 'hits' (Columbia Jacquard page, Britannica punched-card) are pure source-domain references with no LLM-side equivalent of the proposed mechanism; functional-equivalence judge scores remain very low. Novelty claim is upheld: no result proposes lockstep prefix-coherent per-decoder-position activation masking.
